title: biscutella apula subsp. lyrata
intro: Biscutella apula subsp. lyrata, also known as a type of rock cudweed, is a flowering plant belonging to the Brassicaceae family. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is rich in vitamins and antioxidants, offering health benefits like reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Biscutella apula subsp. lyrata can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and its leaves can also be consumed as a fresh addition to salads or used in cooking.
